entries.title: Anthropic co-founder confirms the company briefed the Trump administration on Mythos
entries.description: In an interview at the Semafor World Economy summit this week, Anthropic co-founder Jack Clark explained why the company was still engaged with the U.S. government while simultaneously suing them.

entries.title: Google adds AI Skills to Chrome to help you save favorite workflows
entries.description: Google is adding “Skills” to Chrome, letting users save and reuse AI prompts across websites. The feature builds on Gemini’s browser integration.

entries.title: In just a couple weeks, StrictlyVC San Francisco brings leaders from TDK Ventures, Replit, and more together
entries.description:
The first StrictlyVC of the year will be coming to San Francisco before you know it.

There are still a few tickets available to join us and our stacked speaker lineup April 30, so register today!

entries.title: Google brings its Gemini Personal Intelligence feature to India
entries.description: The feature lets users connect Google accounts like Gmail and Photos to get personalized answers.

entries.title: How vibe-coding app Anything is rebuilding after getting booted from the App Store twice
entries.description: Anything is planning to launch a desktop companion app to aid mobile app development after its App Store removal.

entries.title: OpenAI has bought AI personal finance startup Hiro
entries.description: The acquisition indicates a capability that OpenAI is building into ChatGPT: financial planning.

entries.title: Trump officials may be encouraging banks to test Anthropic’s Mythos model
entries.description: The report is particularly surprising since the Department of Defense recently declared Anthropic a supply-chain risk.

entries.title: Apple reportedly testing four designs for upcoming smart glasses
entries.description: These glasses are a step back from an ambitious plan that once called for Apple to launch a variety of mixed and augmented reality devices.

entries.title: From LLMs to hallucinations, here’s a simple guide to common AI terms
entries.description: The rise of AI has brought an avalanche of new terms and slang. Here is a glossary with definitions of some of the most important words and phrases you might encounter.

entries.description: Anthropic was the star of the show at San Francisco's AI-centric conference.

entries.title: Sam Altman responds to ‘incendiary’ New Yorker article after attack on his home
entries.description: The OpenAI CEO's new blog post responds to both an apparent attack on his home and an in-depth New Yorker profile raising questions about his trustworthiness.

entries.title: Microsoft is working on yet another OpenClaw-like agent
entries.description: The new features would be geared toward enterprise customers, with better security controls than the famously risky open source OpenClaw agent.

entries.title: Stanford report highlights growing disconnect between AI insiders and everyone else
entries.description: Stanford’s latest AI Index shows a widening gap between experts and the public, with rising anxiety over jobs, healthcare, and the economy.

entries.description: Kepler Communications is flying 40 GPUs in Earth orbit. And its latest customer is Sophia Space.

entries.title: Anthropic temporarily banned OpenClaw’s creator from accessing Claude
entries.description: This ban took place after Claude's pricing changed for OpenClaw users last week.

entries.title: Stalking victim sues OpenAI, claims ChatGPT fueled her abuser’s delusions and ignored her warnings
entries.description: OpenAI ignored three warnings that a ChatGPT user was dangerous — including its own mass-casualty flag — while he stalked and harassed his ex-girlfriend, a new lawsuit alleges.
